title: Use the JOG Control on the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen for Manual Tipper Positioning
summary: Use the JOG button on the Visu_MCP_Dual operator station screen to access manual control of the end effector and gripper position. The source explicitly states that only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper, but this source section does not provide the detailed jogging sequence, limits, or recovery actions.
when_to_use: Use when maintenance personnel need to access the documented JOG function on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen to manually control the location and position of the end effector and gripper.
not_for: Do not use for non-maintenance personnel; the source notes that only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.
Do not use as a complete jogging procedure for axis movement, limits, or recovery because those details are not provided in this source section.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and use the documented JOG control for manual positioning.

safety_notes: Only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.
This source section documents the existence and purpose of the JOG control but does not provide detailed motion controls, limits, or recovery actions.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Visu_MCP_Dual screen is available on the operator station HMI

visual_references.description: Operator Station Control Panel showing the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and the JOG button.
Operator station display controls showing screen navigation, including F3 to Visu_MCP_Dual.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and locate JOG
steps.purpose: Reach the documented tipper machine control panel and identify the manual positioning control.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, open the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen and locate the JOG button on the screen.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is displayed and the JOG button is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can identify the JOG button on the Visu_MCP_Dual control panel.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the JOG button cannot be identified from the available screen.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen.

actions.label: Locate JOG button
actions.exact_action: Locate the JOG button on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The JOG button on the Operator Station Control Panel (Figure 4-19).
Screen navigation controls showing access to Visu_MCP_Dual, including F3.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is not available on the operator station HMI.
The JOG button cannot be located on the displayed control panel.
The person performing the task is not maintenance personnel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The JOG button allows the operator to manually control the location and position of the end effector and gripper. NOTE: Only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.
The listed control mapping includes F3 to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.

steps.title: Select JOG for manual positioning control
steps.purpose: Enter the documented manual-control mode for positioning the end effector and gripper.
steps.instruction: Select JOG on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen to access manual control of the location and position of the end effector and gripper.
steps.expected_result: The documented JOG function is selected for manual positioning control.
steps.healthy_condition: JOG is selected and manual control access is available for the end effector and gripper.
steps.failure_condition: Selecting JOG does not provide access to the documented manual control function.

actions.label: Select JOG
actions.exact_action: Select JOG to access manual control of the location and position of the end effector and gripper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The JOG control on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI does not present the documented manual control function after JOG is selected.
The person performing the task is not maintenance personnel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The JOG button allows the operator to manually control the location and position of the end effector and gripper. NOTE: Only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.

steps.title: Limit use to the documented JOG function
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported operation beyond what this source section documents.
steps.instruction: Use only the source-documented JOG function for manual positioning. Do not assume additional movement controls, limits, or recovery actions from this section because they are not described here.
steps.expected_result: Manual positioning is limited to the documented JOG access described by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The user relies only on the documented JOG function and recognizes that detailed jogging controls are not provided in this section.
steps.failure_condition: The user attempts to infer or apply undocumented movement controls, limits, or recovery actions from this source section.

actions.label: Confirm source limitation
actions.exact_action: Use only the source-documented JOG function for manual positioning; no additional movement controls are described in this section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Detailed jog controls, limits, or recovery actions are needed to continue.
There is uncertainty about how to perform actual axis movement safely using only this section.
A reviewer determines the procedure requires additional source-supported detail before use.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The JOG button allows the operator to manually control the location and position of the end effector and gripper. NOTE: Only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.

success_criteria: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is accessed on the operator station HMI.
The JOG button is identified and selected.
The documented manual-control mode for positioning the end effector and gripper is accessed.
Use is limited to maintenance personnel.
healthy_conditions: The JOG button is visible on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
Selecting JOG provides access to the documented manual positioning function.
The user understands that this source section does not define detailed jogging controls.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed.
The JOG button cannot be located or selected.
A non-maintenance user attempts to jog the tipper.
Detailed movement, limit, or recovery instructions are required but not available in this source section.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the person performing the task is not maintenance personnel.
Escalate if the HMI does not match the documented Visu_MCP_Dual/JOG layout.
Escalate for SME review before operational use because this source section does not provide the detailed jog sequence, limits, or recovery actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: JOG button and surrounding controls on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
Navigation controls used to reach Visu_MCP_Dual, including F3.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The JOG button allows the operator to manually control the location and position of the end effector and gripper. NOTE: Only maintenance personnel should jog the tipper.

missing_details: This source section does not provide the detailed jog sequence after selecting JOG.
This source section does not provide axis-specific controls, movement directions, limits, or stop conditions.
This source section does not provide recovery actions if manual positioning fails.
This source section does not state whether production must be stopped before using JOG.
This source section does not state whether lockout/tagout is required.
